speaker: charlie
text: Now here's a story that'll blow your mind Jamie - scientists just won the 3 million dollar Breakthrough Prize for their work on muons. These tiny particles are 207 times heavier than electrons but only exist for a fraction of a second before they decay.

speaker: alice
text: The really cool bit is they've been working on this for over 60 years across three different laboratories - CERN, Brookhaven, and Fermilab. They've created the world's most precise measurement of how these mysterious particles behave, and it might help us understand physics beyond what we currently know.

speaker: charlie
text: It's incredible when you think about it - generations of scientists working together to measure something that exists for microseconds. The ceremony was held in Santa Monica and they're calling it the 'Oscars of Science.' Shows that patience and teamwork in science really does pay off, literally!
